what_it_does: Defines KIND_META, a single dictionary that maps machine event kind identifiers to operator-facing name and is descriptions, and documents how unseeded kinds fall back to a humanized id.
observations: A single module-level KIND_META dictionary holds roughly 50 event-kind entries.
Each entry maps a machine kind id to a dict with name (short label) and is (plain-English explanation).
Comments frame the file as an interpretive layer for a ledger of code/design substrates, kept neutral and file-local.
The registry is meant to be declared-first for a tapped sector; unseeded kinds fall back to a humanized id split on . _ - / + title-case.
The meaning is intended to live in the data, not the instrument; the operator sees only name and is.
Entries are seeded from live store and runtime emit-sites, and a validate/backfill set-diff is supposed to catch missing kinds.
The file is marked TENTATIVE and awaits Tim/DNA ratification; the field-set is journey-gated.
Two live kinds (config_writer.git, projection.verify) are intentionally omitted to avoid fabricating meaning.
The copy was tightened for non-technical readers, translating insider words into plain English.
The journey.* trio was corrected to reflect that the system records a path, not that a user takes a tour.
standouts: An explicit evidence rule: no meaning is fabricated for kinds without an emit-site.
Operator-surface separation: the machine kind-id is hidden from the operator.
Declared-first rendering plus a humanized fallback for unknown kinds.
Two kinds are deliberately left to the fallback rather than invented.
conventions: One data map instead of many files.
Keys are dot-namespaced machine identifiers; values are minimal dicts with name and is.
Extensive timestamped, tentative, and ratification-related comments.
Values use plain-English, non-technical phrasing aimed at operators.
concerns: The registry is still TENTATIVE and not ratified.
Some kind ids overlap or are near-duplicates (run vs op.run; cognition.role.fire/ran vs cognition.turn.start/done), which could confuse operators.
No validation logic is present in the file; coverage depends on an external set-diff.
Fallback humanization may be imprecise for the two intentionally omitted kinds.
notes: A human-readable event-kind registry that keeps operator-facing labels in data, with a safe fallback for kinds not yet seeded.
questions: How exactly is the fallback humanization implemented (tokenization and title-case rules)?
Which component loads this registry and performs the set-diff validation against live kinds?
What is the precise contract with bindings/raw.py beyond sharing the same legibility-type meta shape?
Are the two intentionally omitted kinds still present in live data, and is the fallback acceptable for them?
What gates the journey from TENTATIVE to ratified?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it claims to be a single declared-first registry of human meaning for event kinds, and the file is exactly that dictionary plus design comments.
intention_signals: To let non-technical operators understand what each machine event kind means without the instrument hard-coding labels, by centralizing human-readable descriptions in a validated, fallback-enabled data registry.
novelty: The interpretive layer is stored separately from the binding data, uses id-tokenization as a fallback for unknown kinds, and explicitly refuses to invent meaning for kinds lacking an emit-site.
contribution: A centralized, evidence-grounded semantic map for event kinds that a fused system can keep to render operator-facing labels and to check coverage against live kinds.
summary_for_embedding: company/kinds/raw.py defines KIND_META, a dictionary mapping machine event kind identifiers to operator-facing name and is descriptions, with comments describing declared-first rendering and a humanized fallback for unseeded kinds.
intention_for_embedding: To centralize human meaning for event kinds so instruments can render legible operator labels from data rather than from code.

symbols.does: Module-level dictionary mapping event kind strings to human-readable name and is descriptions.
symbols.description: KIND_META is the core registry of the file: a flat dict whose keys are machine event kind identifiers and whose values are small dicts containing a short name and a longer plain-English is sentence. It is intended to be read first by an instrument to render operator-facing labels, while unseeded kinds fall back to a humanized version of the id.
